common_assumptions: File contents and process state unchanged since last read
Tool output is complete and not silently truncated
Prior session environment (processes, services, auth) still valid after context gaps
Type-checking or compile success implies runtime correctness
Sequential edits across a long chain are each individually correct without intermediate test runs
strength_patterns: Systematic use of project-specific diagnostic scripts (statusline-diag.py, fleet-triage, detect.py) to verify actual runtime behavior before declaring success
Reading surrounding file context and cross-referencing multiple related files before making structural changes
Iterative narrowing via targeted rg searches before making existence or architectural claims
weakness_patterns: Fixation loops: re-reading the same file or re-running the same diagnostic 10-30+ times across a chain without advancing state (f3ddfd2f, fa0b19cf, fc4190a7)
Long tool-call chains (40-135 steps) proceeding without mid-chain verification steps, matching the collect-not-run and declared-ready-without-exercise anti-patterns
Assuming external process, service, and authentication state is unchanged across context boundaries and session resumptions without re-verification
